current_fact.fact_text: On this day in 1969, Apollo 10 launched as a full dress rehearsal for the first Moon landing. The crew flew their lunar module to just 15.6 km above the surface, skimming the lunar landscape in a daring test that paved the way for Apollo 11.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

current_fact.match_reason: A lunar ‘dress rehearsal’ aligns with a themed journey towards a distant world; the episode’s voyage-through-space vibe echoes Apollo 10’s pathfinding mission.

queue.fact_text: In 1953, pilot Jacqueline Cochran became the first woman to break the sound barrier. Flying an F-86 Sabre jet over California, she shattered both the air around her and a major barrier in aviation history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

queue.match_reason: Breaking the sound barrier is an explosive moment of speed and energy; the episode’s ‘electric shock’ theme and high-voltage rock parallel that sonic breakthrough.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1990, a modified French TGV train set a world speed record for rail travel at 515.3 km/h (320.2 mph). For a brief moment, steel, wire, and engineering let a passenger train move faster than many small aircraft.

episode.title: Sedna FM #05 - On The Go - Zero Zero
episode.description: An energetic mix for when you're on the move. Zero Zero captures that feeling of starting fresh, moving forward, and embracing the journey. Perfect for travel, walks, or just getting things done.
songs: Grace Jones - Pull up to the bumper
Easy star all star - Soul love
James Brown - Talking loud and saying nothing (remix)

queue.match_reason: A record‑breaking high‑speed train embodies motion and momentum; this ‘on the go’ travel mix mirrors the sense of acceleration and forward movement.

queue.fact_text: In 1912, "Shree Pundalik"—a silent film about a saintly devotee—was released in Mumbai. Though technically simple, this modest feature is often credited with igniting the sprawling, colorful story of Indian cinema.

episode.title: Morning Drops #38 - History of Flamenco
episode.description: A special journey into the history of Flamenco, guided by Yasmin and her guest Almudena from Madrid, Spain. Exploring the roots and evolution of this emotionally raw and culturally rich tradition, from its early vocal origins and Andalusian influences, through its transformation on stage and screen, to its modern reinventions by iconic Spanish artists.
songs: La Niña de los Peines - Al Gurugú
Paco de Lucía - Entre dos aguas
Camarón de la Isla - La leyenda del tiempo

queue.match_reason: A foundational moment in Indian cinema pairs well with an episode tracing the roots and evolution of Flamenco; both explore how powerful cultural traditions begin modestly and grow into major art forms.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1499, navigator Alonso de Ojeda sailed from Cádiz on a voyage that would bring Europeans to the coasts of what is now Venezuela. His journey blended bold seamanship, mythic cartography, and the first recorded use of the name that would become a country.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: Ojeda’s voyage toward unknown shores resonates with a themed ‘moving meditation’ on travel and perspective shifts; both are journeys that change how the world is seen.

queue.fact_text: In 1695, the Linfen earthquake struck Shanxi Province during China’s Qing dynasty, causing catastrophic destruction and killing at least 52,000 people. The disaster reshaped local settlements and became part of a long seismic memory in northern China.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

queue.match_reason: A devastating earthquake evokes themes of upheaval and rebuilding; this reflective, hopeful episode about weathering storms and moving forward offers an emotional counterpoint to natural catastrophe.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1803, Britain revoked the short-lived Treaty of Amiens and declared war on Napoleonic France, ending a fragile peace that had lasted barely a year. The decision plunged Europe into more than a decade of near-continuous conflict.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

queue.match_reason: The sudden collapse of peace in Europe was a political ‘bomb’ that reshaped history; the raw, explosive energy of the debut Sedna episode mirrors that sense of rupture and new era.

queue.fact_text: In 1794, the Battle of Tourcoing unfolded during the War of the First Coalition, as French Revolutionary forces outmaneuvered a larger allied army in the Low Countries. The victory helped secure the revolution’s northern frontier against monarchist powers.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

queue.match_reason: A revolutionary army fighting monarchist coalitions reflects themes of resistance and confrontation; Rage Against the Machine’s politically charged soundscape amplifies that spirit of defiance.

queue.fact_text: In 1804, Napoleon Bonaparte was proclaimed Emperor of the French by the Senate, turning a revolutionary general into an imperial ruler. The move fused republican rhetoric with monarchical ceremony, redefining power in post-revolutionary Europe.

episode.title: Morning Drops #30 - Star Treatment
episode.description: A celebration of change and transformation. Gaia traces the evolution of Arctic Monkeys - from raw energy to cinematic sound - reflecting on how artists, and all of us, grow and change through time. A journey through courage, curiosity, and the beauty of slowing down.
songs: Arctic Monkeys - There’d Better Be a Mirrorball

queue.match_reason: Napoleon’s transformation from general to emperor is a dramatic evolution of identity and public image; the episode’s focus on artistic transformation and 'star treatment' parallels this shift into a grander persona.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1302, the Bruges Matins saw Flemish militiamen launch a nocturnal surprise attack that massacred the French garrison in Bruges. The brutal uprising became a symbol of local resistance against foreign rule in medieval Flanders.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance. Music shaped by sand, memory, and survival.

queue.match_reason: The Flemish uprising and Tuareg music both channel resistance to domination; this episode’s focus on exile and struggle echoes the rebellious spirit of the Bruges Matins.

queue.fact_text: In 1926, famed evangelist Aimee Semple McPherson mysteriously disappeared from a California beach, sparking nationwide headlines and wild speculation. Weeks later she reappeared with a kidnapping tale that many believed—and others loudly doubted.

episode.title: Sedna FM #06 - Alters of an Ego
episode.description: What if you could step outside yourself and become someone freer, bolder, or completely unexpected? Exploring the artistry of musicians who craft alter-egos and parallel realities to experiment with new dimensions of their identity. It's not about hiding who they are — it's about expanding who they can be.
songs: The Fireman - Strawberries Oceans Ships Forest

queue.match_reason: McPherson’s disappearance and contested narrative blur the line between reality and performance; an episode about alter-egos and parallel identities complements the mystery around who she really was in that missing time.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1944, German paratroopers finally evacuated Monte Cassino after months of ferocious fighting and four major Allied offensives. The battle left a historic monastery in ruins but opened the road to Rome for the Allies.

episode.title: Morning Drops #21 - Endless Summer
episode.description: Recorded from Fano, a quiet town on the Adriatic coast where summer stretches long and slow. Between swims, simple meals, and time with family, Gaia reflects on the kind of music that helps us return to ourselves. Through different shades of jazz — from minimal and meditative to warm and rhythmic — an invitation to slip into that endless summer feeling.

queue.match_reason: The end of a grinding mountain battle and the opening of a path south contrasts sharply with calm Adriatic summers; the gentle jazz and coastal reflections offer a meditative counter-image to wartime destruction in Italy.

queue.fact_text: In 1980, Mount St. Helens erupted catastrophically in Washington State, blowing off its summit, triggering massive landslides, and sending ash across the continent. The eruption reshaped the volcano, the surrounding ecosystems, and the way scientists monitor restless mountains.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

queue.match_reason: A violent volcanic eruption and serene Arctic seas both show Earth’s raw power; this cinematic, nature-focused episode offers a contemplative way to feel the planet’s dramatic forces and recoveries.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1974, India carried out its first nuclear test under the code name "Smiling Buddha" in the Rajasthan desert. The paradoxical name masked a seismic political shift: India had quietly joined the club of nuclear-armed states.

episode.title: Morning Drops #48 - Cosmic Monday
episode.description:
Cosmic Monday is a Morning Drops episode built around four Italian dance records, tracing how disco rhythms slowly became electronic language.
Gaia moves through four tracks like chapters: groove first, then machines, then that bright, futuristic feeling you only get when the bassline is doing the talking. It's not nostalgia, exactly. It's more like returning to a sound that still works — especially on mornings when you need a little extra energy, but you don't want anything aggressive.
Take it as a slow transmission. Let it carry you into the day.
songs: Giovanotti Mondani Meccanici - Don't Ask Me Why

queue.match_reason: A secret nuclear test with a serene name blends power, technology, and paradox; the episode’s sleek, futuristic electronics and cosmic vibe mirror the unsettling mix of modernity and hidden force.

queue.fact_text: In 1991, northern Somalia declared itself the independent Republic of Somaliland after the collapse of the central government. Though unrecognized by most of the world, Somaliland built its own institutions, elections, and sense of nationhood in the Horn of Africa.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

queue.match_reason: Somaliland’s self-declared statehood is a story of African self-determination; an episode celebrating Afrobeat’s African-rooted power and independence complements this narrative of forging one’s own path.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1993, protests against Denmark’s Maastricht Treaty opt-outs turned violent in Copenhagen’s Nørrebro district. Police opened fire on demonstrators, marking the first time since World War II that Danish authorities shot civilians in a domestic disturbance.

episode.title: Morning Drops #08 - (un)Boxing
episode.description: An episode about breaking free from expectations and labels. Music that helps us unbox ourselves and embrace who we truly are.
songs: Kendrick Lamar - The Blacker the Berry

queue.match_reason: The Nørrebro riots were partly about identity, sovereignty, and being boxed in by supranational rules; an episode centered on breaking out of boxes and confronting uncomfortable truths resonates with that tension.

queue.fact_text: In 1980, civil unrest spread through Miami’s predominantly African-American neighborhoods of Overtown and Liberty City after an all-white jury acquitted police officers in the killing of Arthur McDuffie. The riots exposed deep racial inequities and police-community tensions in the city.

episode.title: Morning Drops #04 - Bars With Balls
episode.description: Yasmin dives into Women's Day through the voices of four incredible female rappers. Morning fire from the magic planet of Sedna FM — move your hands up and down with us. Hip hop culture through a feminine lens.
songs: Little Simz - Point and Kill (feat. Obongjayar)

queue.match_reason: Riots over racial injustice in Miami connect with hip-hop’s long tradition of challenging inequality; this episode’s powerful rap voices, especially Queen Latifah’s “U.N.I.T.Y.,” amplify themes of resistance and dignity.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 2018, Cubana de Aviación Flight 972 crashed shortly after takeoff from Havana’s José Martí International Airport, killing 112 of the 113 people on board. The accident was one of Cuba’s deadliest air disasters and prompted scrutiny of aging aircraft and leasing practices.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

queue.match_reason: A tragic plane crash invites somber reflection on fragility and recovery; this reflective episode about healing after sudden disruption offers a sensitive space to process such loss.

queue.fact_text: In 2006, Nepal’s post-revolution parliament passed a landmark bill that stripped the king of most powers and declared the country a secular state. The move marked a seismic shift away from centuries of Hindu monarchy toward republican democracy.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

queue.match_reason: Nepal’s transition from monarchy to a more self-determined, secular democracy reflects a society rethinking its structures; an episode about rethinking our relationship with technology mirrors this broader theme of reassessing power and dependence.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1990, Northern Somalia’s later path to declaring Somaliland began to crystallize amid negotiations and conflict; a year later, the region would formally announce its separation. The seeds of a de facto state were being sown as central authority disintegrated.

episode.title: Morning Drops #35 - Thailand
episode.description: Recorded in Koh Samui, Thailand, where Yasmin and her friend Patricia spent more than two intense weeks training Muay Thai and Western boxing. Between sweat, rhythm, and sea breeze, they share reflections on discipline, friendship, and the beauty of movement - with a soundtrack that connects Thai sounds to the flow of daily practice.
songs: Burna Boy - Cheat on Me (feat. Dave)

queue.match_reason: As central structures crumbled and local resilience began shaping Somaliland’s future, themes of discipline, adaptation, and inner strength in the Thailand training episode provide a metaphorical parallel for building something new under pressure.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 2005, Hubble’s confirmation of Pluto’s moons Nix and Hydra quietly expanded the cast of characters in our solar system’s distant frontier. Each new icy companion helps astronomers piece together the chaotic history of Pluto’s family.

episode.title: Evening Flows #00 - Introduction to evening flows
episode.description:
Sedna FM introduces a new format: Evening Flow, where you can enjoy curated music sets from selectors, DJs, and artists.

Each mix is an invitation to drift beyond the expected. A space where hidden gems from personal archives come to light, where sound speaks its own language. There are no rules here, no patterns to follow. Just the steady unfolding of a mood, a texture, a journey shaped by feeling.

Let the music lead the way - enjoy the trip!

queue.match_reason: New moons appearing at the dark edge of the solar system match the feeling of discovering hidden sounds after sunset; this open-ended Evening Flow intro invites listeners to drift outward, just as Hubble’s gaze does.

queue.fact_text: In 1912, while "Shree Pundalik" flickered in an Indian theater, elsewhere the age of film was stitching together a new global language of moving images. Each early reel was an experiment in how stories, light, and technology could reshape culture.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

queue.match_reason: Early cinema and trip-hop share a cinematic sensibility and experimental layering of images or sounds; this episode’s filmic, boundary-blurring music fits the birth of a new visual storytelling language.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1953, while Jacqueline Cochran pierced the sound barrier, engineers around the world were racing to understand the physics of transonic flight—how shockwaves cling to wings and tail surfaces. Cracking this problem opened the door to the jet age and routine high-speed travel.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

queue.match_reason: Overcoming the sound barrier required years of trial, error, and scientific learning; an episode celebrating the slow, effortful joy of learning pairs well with this story of persistence reshaping what is possible in the sky.

published.fact_text: In 2005, astronomers using the Hubble Space Telescope confirmed that Pluto is orbited by two tiny additional moons, later named Nix and Hydra. Their discovery hinted that even the solar system’s outcasts hold complex miniature systems of their own.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A fact about new moons around Pluto fits a mindset of expanding ‘space’ and possibility; the episode’s title and cosmic metaphor mirror the discovery of hidden worlds in the outer solar system.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1969, Apollo 10 launched as a full dress rehearsal for the first Moon landing. The crew flew their lunar module to just 15.6 km above the surface, skimming the lunar landscape in a daring test that paved the way for Apollo 11.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

published.match_reason: A lunar ‘dress rehearsal’ aligns with a themed journey towards a distant world; the episode’s voyage-through-space vibe echoes Apollo 10’s pathfinding mission.
